description: Demonstrates the r-index repair for pair IBP derivative-order alignment. The wrong candidate sets r_primitive = r_target; the source-backed candidate sets r_primitive = r_target - 1.
scientific_context: In integration-by-parts reduction of the pair term, the derivative operator ∂_k P G^η_r maps between r-indexed and (r+1)-indexed primitives. The correct alignment is r_primitive = r_target - 1, meaning the primitive at order r-1 produces G^η_r under the derivative ∂_k. The wrong historical candidate used r_primitive = r_target.

domain_adaptation.original_domain: sigma_xxx pair IBP: ∂_k P G^η_r = α_η v_a P G^η_{r+1}
domain_adaptation.adapted_domain: Compact domain-adapted fixture preserving the structural r-index alignment logic without requiring the full sigma_xxx expression
domain_adaptation.adaptation_rule: The fixture uses a simplified symbolic structure that captures the essential derivative-index alignment property

wrong_alignment.ss_signal: exact nonzero residual in derived identity

repaired_alignment.ss_signal: target derivative image aligned with primitive
